untranslatable_reason: This signal requires options-specific data (near-term vs longer-dated option expiration values, spread debit/credit comparisons, and implied volatility decay) which are not available in the standard price/technical indicator set. Conditions like 'near-term options expire worthless', 'longer-dated options retain value exceeding initial debit', and 'selling longer-dated options value > initial spread debit' cannot be expressed using only OHLCV and technical indicators. A dedicated options backtester with Greeks and time-decay modeling is required.
translation_notes: Signal describes an advanced calendar spread/diagonal spread exit strategy specific to options trading. The entry condition was mapped to RANGING regime as specified. The exit was approximated as a trend reversal signal, but this is a poor substitute for actual options expiration and value tracking. This rule requires options chain data and should not be used with equity price data alone.
